page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-48 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance powered by the t2a family. It features 48 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 192 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth. The stress-ng multi-core test yields a strong score of 87,022.37, while memory bandwidth tests show strong read-only performance at 3,391.09 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 181.23 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Conversely, single-core performance is relatively modest, as evidenced by a weak Geekbench single-core score of 1,110.0 and a poor text generation speed of 19.46 tokens/sec on small language models. Redis SET operations perform strongly at 61.65 million operations per second.

The t2a-standard-48 offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency due to its dedicated physical cores and ARM architecture, which avoids the overhead of hyperthreading. It is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads, web serving, database operations, and general-purpose applications that can leverage parallel processing. However, it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or heavy LLM text generation workloads due to its lower single-core performance.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-48 is an ARM-based general-purpose server featuring 48 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture clocked at 3.0 GHz. Equipped with 192 GB of RAM, it maintains a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 87,022.37 and high memory read bandwidth. However, single-core performance is weaker, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1,110.0. This hardware profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads, making it suitable for multi-threaded applications, Redis databases, and static web serving, while being less effective for single-threaded tasks or large language model text generation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-48 is an ARM-based server with 48 dedicated physical cores and 192 GB RAM, offering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-48 is an ARM-based server featuring 48 dedicated physical cores and 192 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose instance featuring forty-eight dedicated physical cores and balanced memory for highly parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 3.0 GHz
192 GB of system memory with a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
Strong multi-core processing and high memory read bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, Redis databases, and web serving
